title: euphrasia minima subsp. frigida
intro: Euphrasia minima subsp. frigida, also known as the Arctic Eyebright, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Orobanchaceae, native to high-altitude and Arctic regions. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to treat eye and respiratory issues due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Euphrasia minima subsp. frigida include its potential to relieve conjunctivitis, sinusitis, and other inflammatory conditions, as well as its antimicrobial properties that can help combat infections. Herbal preparations of this plant include infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be used to treat various health issues, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
